subject: one last thing:
body:
Kirsten and I are heading over to CNN in just a few hours.

name: KG
subject: a fundamental question
body:
How we share our vision on the debate stage:
[](https://secure.kirstengillibrand.com/page/m/4f976e1f/2ae112ef/6c66c3ca/357dcc78/2166269464/VEsH/ "https://2020.kirstengillibrand.com/")

Peter, this moment in history is challenging us to answer a fundamental question: **Will brave win?**
It hasn't always--and it isn't right now. As long as Donald Trump sits in the Oval Office, we will see leadership rooted in nothing but fear, cowardice and bigotry. We will see greed and self-interest take priority over what's best for you.
**But I know that brave will win as long as we join together to fight for our values --and the first step is getting on the national debate stage to share our agenda with the entire country. **[We can guarantee our spot in the debates with donations from 65,000 individual Americans. Giving any amount is the single most important thing you can do to power our movement, raise our voices and defeat Trump.](https://secure.kirstengillibrand.com/page/m/4f976e1f/2ae112ef/6c66c3ca/357dcc7b/2166269464/VEsF/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5Ijoia2ctZW1haWwiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6Imp0azMwMjUtZnItZ2ZwLW5kIiwiZTNzZ1RGQkJRVkYxWlhKNVUzUnlhVzVuSUgxOSI6IjUsMjUsNTAsMTAwLDI1MCw1MDAiLCJlM3NnWVdKeVpXTjFjbkpwYm1jZ2ZYMD0iOiIifQ==/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/kg-email?refcode=jtk3025-fr-gfp-nd&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500")****

**At last night 's CNN town hall and on the campaign trail, I've laid out my vision of what brave will look like with me in the White House:**
* We'll create a publicly funded election system and pass a national automatic voter-registration policy;
* We'll take on the NRA to finally end gun violence in our country;
* We'll pass a Green New Deal to save our planet from the impacts of climate change;
* We'll build a public school system where every last student can get a quality education;
* You'll have a president that you can count on to put you and your family first every time.

That's what we can achieve if we're brave together. And it's the vision I'll share from the Democratic debate stage. But I can't guarantee my spot unless we reach the 65,000 individual donors we need to get there.

This is a moment when we all need to give absolutely everything to the fights ahead. Because if we stand up, lock arms and do this together, brave _will_ win.
I'm so grateful for all of your support.
--KG

body:

---
| | |
# **Democrats’ Open Border Fantasy Vision is a Lie – We Have a Humanitarian Crisis to Fix**

“The dramatically worsening crisis at the border – the unfolding humanitarian disaster and utter breakdown of any semblance of the rule of law – is the inevitable result of the open borders agenda of the political class in Washington,” Brett Velicovich writes in Fox News.

“Right now, we’re getting a real picture of what open borders looks like: an unbridled flood of humanity that our hamstrung law enforcement officers are powerless to stop.”

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=cf6e8fcefb&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| President Donald J. Trump travels to Houston, Texas, today to announce executive orders aimed at speeding up pipeline and other energy projects and expanding American energy production, James Osborne writes in the _[Houston Chronicle](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=c524088fdf&e=3ee4985eea)_. "With U.S. crude oil production -- now at more than 12 million barrels a day -- surpassing both Saudi Arabia and Russia, it has provided the administration more leeway in targeting sanctions at oil producers such as Iran and Venezuela without fearing a spike in oil prices."
---
|
---
| “Law enforcement agents apprehended or turned back 103,492 migrants attempting to reach the U.S. southern border in March, marking the highest month in 12 years,” Jason Hopkins writes in _[The Daily Caller](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8588e99e48&e=3ee4985eea)_. "'The Border Patrol is facing an unprecedented humanitarian and border security crisis,' Brian Hastings, Border Patrol’s chief of law enforcement operations, told reporters Tuesday. 'We’ve arrived at the breaking point.'"
---
|
---
| “The collusion lie will go down in history as one of the strangest distortions of reality to dominate the American political scene. For more than two years, the national establishment and news media were fixated on a ‘truth’ that turned out to be false,” former Speaker of the House Newt Gingrich writes in [Fox News](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=5152e0f9a6&e=3ee4985eea). Consider the volume of fake news coverage: “The New York Times, The Washington Post, CNN, and MSNBC combined published 8,507 articles citing Mueller’s investigation since May 2017 – nearly 13 articles every single day.”
---
|
---
| “A northern Texas sheriff says his county has seen a big uptick in methamphetamine and heroin seizures since October, saying Mexican drug smugglers are using large groups of migrants to divert Border Patrol’s attention while they run narcotics over the border in nearby areas,” Anna Giaritelli writes for the _[Washington Examiner](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=ba793bd2ee&e=3ee4985eea)_.
---

name: Bernie Sanders
subject: I’m asking you to add your name to say you support my Medicare for All bill
body:
It is past time to guarantee health care as a right for all.
# [](https://act.berniesanders.com/go/8915?t=1&akid=1011%2E150750%2EOxQtjS)

Sisters, Brothers, and Friends -
It was only a few years ago that the corporate media and political establishment laughed us off when we said it was past time to guarantee health care as a right for all.
Well, that’s not the case anymore. Because right now Medicare for All is not only supported by the majority of Americans, it is also an idea embraced by many Democrats, including most running for president.
Let me be very clear: that did not happen by accident. It happened because of you. Because of us.
Together we have made incredible progress on this issue, but we are not finished yet.
Today I am reintroducing Medicare for All legislation in the Senate. And it's important that we send an unmistakable message to my colleagues that health care must be guaranteed as a right for all Americans.
I cannot do it alone. So I am asking you directly:
**[Add your name to say you support my Medicare-for-All, single-payer health care bill. Health care is a human right, not a privilege.](https://act.berniesanders.com/signup/bernie_mfa_cosponsor_april/?source=em190410-1-120&t=2&akid=1011%2E150750%2EOxQtjS)**
It will come as no surprise that the health insurance and pharmaceutical companies do not like our Medicare for All plan.
They would rather continue making billions in profits off a dysfunctional health care system that leaves millions of Americans struggling to get the care and prescription drugs that they need.
It has never made sense to me that our health care system is primarily designed to make huge profits for multibillion-dollar insurance companies, drug companies, hospitals, and medical equipment suppliers. Health care is not a commodity. It is a human right. And the goal of a sane health care system should be to keep people well, not to make stockholders rich.
So today we say to the private health insurance companies, whether they like it or not, the United States will join every other major country on earth and guarantee health care to all people as a right. All Americans are entitled to go to the doctor when they're sick and not go bankrupt after staying in the hospital.
If we are serious about providing high-quality and affordable health care to every man, woman, and child in this country, the only real solution is a Medicare for All, single-payer system that provides health care to ALL Americans.
Although we have seen growing support in Congress, the truth is that we are still taking on much of the political and financial establishment in this fight. There are many who still believe universal health care is a radical idea. But what they will soon realize is that this is a struggle whose time has come.
**Today, as I re-introduce legislation in the Senate, we must send a powerful message that we are prepared to fight for – and win – Medicare for All. The only way we will win is if we are united and prepared to fight on this issue.**
Let's show we're in this together:
**[Add your name in support of my Medicare for All bill if you believe it's past time for our country to guarantee health care as a right.](https://act.berniesanders.com/signup/bernie_mfa_cosponsor_april/?source=em190410-1-120&t=3&akid=1011%2E150750%2EOxQtjS)**
Our ideas are winning, and today it is important that we show we are as committed to this fight as ever.
In solidarity,
Bernie Sanders
* * *
**Today I am re-introducing legislation in the Senate, and I need your help to send an unmistakable message that health care must be granted as a right to all.

Add your name to say you support our Medicare-for-All, single-payer health care bill.
**
[ADD YOUR NAME](https://act.berniesanders.com/signup/bernie_mfa_cosponsor_april/?source=em190410-1-120&t=4&akid=1011%2E150750%2EOxQtjS)

Paid for by Bernie 2020
PO BOX 391, Burlington, VT 05402
This email was sent to **dpwillis67@gmail.com**. If you need to, [**unsubscribe here**](https://bernie.actionkit.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe?t=5&akid=1011%2E150750%2EOxQtjS). You can also [sign up to receive fewer emails](https://act.berniesanders.com/signup/fewer-emails/?t=6&akid=1011%2E150750%2EOxQtjS). We hope you'll stay, because emails are such an important part of how we organize and how we raise money. In fact, there is no single greater source for our campaign's fundraising than emails like this one. And that's important, because no one person, not even Bernie Sanders, can take on Trump and the billionaire class alone.

Before you go about the rest of your day, help power our fight against the billionaire class by [making a contribution today](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bernie-email-template?refcode=em-footer&t=7&refcode2=1011_150750_OxQtjS&akid=1011%2E150750%2EOxQtjS).
Submit this form to [update your contact information](https://act.berniesanders.com/signup/update-info/?t=8&akid=1011%2E150750%2EOxQtjS).

Nunes is shamelessly going after anyone and everyone who embarrasses him. It might seem funny that his feelings are so easily hurt, but make no mistake: **this is a dangerous attack on free speech from a seated member of Congress.**
He's doing the rounds on right-wing talk shows to paint himself as a victim of defamation while parroting Trumpian accusations of "fake news."
Nunes is a Trump lackey and a major opponent of expanding voting rights. It's time to take him out.

body:
Our conservative ideals must bind us more than our gender or the color of our skin. name: Senator Kirsten Gillibrand
subject: Why I signed on to Medicare for All
body:
This morning, I joined my Senate colleagues in introducing Medicare for All.
| | |
---
| | I'm proud to co-sponsor a new Medicare for All bill because I believe health care should be a right guaranteed to all Americans, not a privilege for those who can afford it. **[Add your name if you agree: We need Medicare for All.](https://secure.kirstengillibrand. "https://act.kirstengillibrand.com/kg-medicare-for-all?firstName=Peter&lastName=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500&code=jtk3042-su-gfp-nd")**
---
Peter,
Quality, affordable health care is a right. Not a privilege.
It's as simple as that.
In the wealthiest country in the world, no one should be forced to go without health insurance because it's too expensive. And no American should be sent into medical bankruptcy if their health care costs skyrocket because of an illness or injury.
Our current health care system leaves too many Americans behind. To fix it, we need to invest in big, bold ideas--and Americans deserve a president who will be brave enough to take on this and every tough fight.
That's why I joined my Senate colleagues this morning to introduce a new Medicare for All bill.
**[We can't get this done alone--we need everyone who believes in health care as a universal right to speak out together. Click here to add your name and say you're with me today as I back Medicare for All--because health care should be a right, not a privilege, afforded to every American.](https://secure.kirstengillibrand. "https://act.kirstengillibrand.com/kg-medicare-for-all?firstName=Peter&lastName=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500&code=jtk3042-su-gfp-nd")**

| | [ADD YOUR NAME](https://secure.kirstengillibrand. "https://act.kirstengillibrand.com/kg-medicare-for-all?firstName=Peter&lastName=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500&code=jtk3042-su-gfp-nd")
---

I've been fighting for quality health care my whole career. In my first House race in 2006, I ran in a 2-to-1 majority-Republican district and won on this issue--because expanding health care coverage is a commonsense solution supported by Americans across party lines.
And I wrote the Senate's transition plan to get to Medicare for All from our current system, because it's essential we maintain coverage for millions of Americans while we improve.
Health care is one of the biggest expenses for families across our country, and our health care system still leaves too many Americans hurting. The Trump administration is hellbent on making it worse, with threats to gut protections for people with preexisting conditions and a repeated insistence on rolling back the Affordable Care Act.
Enough is enough: We need Medicare for All to make high-quality, universal, affordable health care a reality for everyone.
**Take a minute to add your name to say you support Medicare for All --just click this link: **
**[_https://act.kirstengillibrand.com/kg-medicare-for-all_](https://secure.kirstengillibrand. "https://act.kirstengillibrand.com/kg-medicare-for-all?firstName=Peter&lastName=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500&code=jtk3042-su-gfp-nd")**
Together, we can build a system that works for all of us.
Thanks,

Kirsten

| The White House • April 10, 2019
---
| | |
# **A $32 trillion, socialist takeover of your healthcare**

**“If you like your insurance plan,** you will keep it. No one will be able to take that away from you. It hasn't happened yet. It won't happen in the future.”

**In April 2010** , former President Barack Obama famously promised Americans that his government overhaul of U.S. healthcare wouldn’t burden their families: They could keep their plans, keep their doctors, and expect to see large savings in their premiums.

**Those all proved to be lies**. Nearly 5 million Americans were told their healthcare plans were going to be canceled in the first year of Obamacare. Average individual market premiums more than doubled from 2013 to 2017. Average premiums on the Federal exchange rose by $2,600 from 2016 to 2017 alone. And even President Obama himself later admitted that families would have to “make choices” to keep their doctors.

**Congressional Democrats lied** to the American people about healthcare once. Now, they’re prepared to do it again.

**Today, self-proclaimed socialist** Sen. Bernie Sanders proposed a total government takeover of American healthcare. Less than a decade after Obamacare was passed, Congressional Democrats are lining up behind this radical vision—a stunning admission of Obamacare’s continued failure to deliver on its core promises to working families.

**“Medicare for All”** is what Democrats call the new plan. It’s a big, messy proposal, but here are the three things that every citizen should know about what it would do:
1. **End private insurance** as we know it. The core of the Medicare-for-All scheme is to force nearly 180 million Americans with private health insurance onto government-run plans. Families would face the threat of losing their doctors and, indeed, any choice at all in their health care decisions.

2. **Seniors** **pay the price.** America’s seniors have been paying their whole lives into a system that promised to take care of them in their old age. But as seen in European single-payer systems, “Medicare for all” will likely lead to less care for seniors and longer wait times. Twenty million Americans would lose their Medicare Advantage Plans.

3. **Cost $32 _trillion_. **Who pays the exorbitant price tag? Working families. According to the Council of Economic Advisers, the tax increases would lower household incomes by $17,000 annually after taxes and healthcare expenditures.

**Democrats are right** that Americans need relief from Obamacare. They are wrong that doubling down on its worst mistakes will make our country stronger. No plan that hurts seniors, kicks 180 million Americans off their health plans, and burdens future generations with unprecedented debt is acceptable. Instead, the Trump Administration is working on realistic solutions to provide Americans with the options they want, the affordability they need, and the quality they deserve.

**[Compare Democrats’ socialist plan with President Trump’s real solutions](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=c8c3d893a3&e=3ee4985eea)**

**More:[Statement from Press Secretary Sarah Sanders ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=90c1485aa8&e=3ee4985eea)**
---
|
---
|
## **The war on American energy is over**

**President Trump** traveled to Houston today, where he signed two important executive orders to continue the revival of American energy dominance.

**Under the Obama Administration,** America had no shortage of energy supply but a huge shortage of the political will to become the world’s energy leader. Instead, crippling regulations stifled production and stalled projects—with the brunt of the pain felt by the workers who lost jobs and the families who were denied affordable electricity and gasoline.

**President Trump took office** with a commitment to responsibly develop our abundant resources and put American energy back on top globally. Now, just two years into his Administration, the United States is the number one producer of oil and natural gas on Earth.

**Today’s actions** continue this upward path by clearing the way for energy infrastructure development. The first order will speed up the approval process for vital energy projects—oil pipelines, roads, and railways—that were frequently blocked by entrenched bureaucrats. The second modernizes outdated regulations for liquefied natural gas export terminals, opening markets for American energy around the globe.

[Watch: President Trump is making American energy number 1 on Earth](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=a6ab39743a&e=3ee4985eea)
